title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text: Map tooltip data_id hover point: shows tooltip dims others. data_id join key; tooltip box says.
code: vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = cyl, tooltip = model, data_id = model) |>   as_widget()

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text:
Tooltip text rendered safe HTML: build multi-line, formatted string tooltip = (glue) using
. Data values escaped, inert tags (, <>,
, ) honoured, injection risk. Style box tooltip_style.
code:
df$label <- glue::glue("{df$model}
{df$mpg} mpg · {df$wt}k lbs") vplot(df) |> mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, tooltip = label, data_id = model) |> as_widget(tooltip_style = list(background = "#1d3557", fontsize = "13px"))

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text: Click mark select (every mark sharing data_id toggles together); drag rectangle brush-select. select_mode = "single" makes click replace selection instead toggling.
code: vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, data_id = model, selected_color = "#e63946") |>   as_widget()

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text: Zoom mouse wheel, drag pan (toggle brush/pan toolbar). touch device, drag pans two-finger pinch zooms; widget focused, arrow keys pan, +/- zoom, 0 resets. -hover toolbar adds zoom--selection, reset, SVG/PNG download, copy--clipboard (supported), fullscreen. Export captures current view, zoomed-region exports shown. Set download name hi-res PNG scale:
code: vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, data_id = model) |>   as_widget(export_filename = "mtcars", export_scale = 2)

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text: Accessibility default (a11y = TRUE). widget announces interactive chart, every mark focusable: Tab chart, use arrow keys move marks (announced polite live region), Enter/Space select, Escape leave traversal mode. visually-hidden data table lists every mark screen-reader users. chart’s accessible name description come plot’s title alt text — quill sets automatically — explicit as_widget(alt =). See quill Accessibility article full cross-package story (alt text, accessible SVG/PDF, widget).

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text: Widgets sharing group link client-side: selecting brushing one highlights data keys others — Shiny, crosstalk. Selection projects hover_group marks declare . interop plotly / leaflet / DT crosstalk’s filter_* controls, pass crosstalk::SharedData (group name) crosstalk = instead.
code: p <- vplot(df) |> mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, data_id = model, hover_group = cyl) htmltools::tagList(   as_widget(p, group = "cars", width = 320, height = 240),   as_widget(vplot(df) |> mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, data_id = model, hover_group = cyl),             group = "cars", width = 320, height = 240) )

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text: hover_color, selected_color, dim_opacity set widget-wide theme; per-mark hover_color / selected_color declared quill override mark. tooltip_style themes tooltip box ().
code: vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, data_id = model) |>   as_widget(hover_color = "seagreen", selected_color = "firebrick", dim_opacity = 0.15)

title: Interactive widgets: a tour
text: glossOutput() / renderGloss() embed widget Shiny app (display-tier today; server-side selection read-back planned). See ?gloss-shiny.

title: Using gloss in Shiny
text: Render widget standard htmlwidgets pair, exactly like plotly DT:
code: library(shiny) library(quill) library(gloss)  ui <- fluidPage(   glossOutput("plot", height = "500px"),   verbatimTextOutput("info") )  server <- function(input, output) {   output$plot <- renderGloss({     vplot(mtcars) |>       mark_point(         x = wt, y = mpg,         tooltip = rownames(mtcars),         data_id = rownames(mtcars) # <- the key each interaction reports       ) |>       as_widget()   })    output$info <- renderPrint({     input$plot_selected # the keys currently selected   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Using gloss in Shiny
text: inputs named <outputId>_<event> — glossOutput("plot") input$plot_selected, input$plot_click, input$plot_hover, input$plot_brush. Every value expressed element keys — data_id set mark — map back data key. State vs event. State inputs (_selected, _hover) update value changes, re-selecting set re-fire — pair observe() / reactive(). Event inputs (_click, _brush) fire every occurrence, even repeat — pair observeEvent().

title: Using gloss in Shiny
text: everything keyed, common patterns one-liners. Filter table brushed points: Show hovered car: Respond click:
code: output$table <- renderTable({   keys <- input$plot_brush$keys   if (length(keys)) mtcars[rownames(mtcars) %in% keys, ] else mtcars[0, ] }) output$detail <- renderText({   k <- input$plot_hover   if (is.null(k)) "Hover a point" else paste(k, "-", mtcars[k, "hp"], "hp") }) observeEvent(input$plot_click, {   key <- input$plot_click$key   if (!is.null(key)) showNotification(paste("clicked", key)) })

title: Using gloss in Shiny
text: data_id (mark key) everything reports; without marks drawn carry identity report. tooltip independent — hover box shows, input carries. inputs fire inside live Shiny session. static render (knitr, pkgdown, htmltools::save_html()) byte--byte identical emits nothing. keys crosstalk SharedData uses, gloss widget can link DT, plotly, leaflet client-side without Shiny — see as_widget(crosstalk=). Shiny inputs crosstalk complementary: use Shiny server needs react, crosstalk pure client-side linking. Driving widget server — setting selection filter without re-rendering — planned addition (gloss_proxy()), yet available. See interactive widgets tour full set client-side interactions, accessibility article keyboard screen-reader model.

what: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
title: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
text: gloss turns vellum scene — quill plot — self-contained, client-side interactive HTML widget: hover tooltips + highlighting, click selection, rectangular brush-select, pan/zoom, toolbar, Shiny server round-trip. host adapter vellum interactivity stack: vellum emits per-element data-keys, bounding boxes, scene_model() element table, quill declares interactive, gloss hosts . Interactions: hover (tooltip + highlight, nearest-mark snapping hover_group linking) · click-select (single/multiple) · drag rectangle brush-select · wheel / pan-drag pan-zoom · discrete-legend interaction (hover swatch highlight series, click select ) · toolbar (mode toggle, zoom--selection, reset, save SVG/PNG, fullscreen) · keyboard + screen-reader access (Tab , arrow keys move marks, Enter/Space select; a11y, default). opt-outable via as_widget() argument. name manuscript gloss — annotation revealed page.

title: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
text: gloss builds vellum backend (works quill plots); pak pulls automatically. vellum compiles Rust crate, Rust toolchain (cargo/rustc) needed build .

title: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
text: as_widget() terminal: compiles plot vellum scene, emits SVG element table, returns htmlwidget. also accepts bare vellum scene. Declare interactivity quill reserved mark arguments data_id (join key), tooltip, hover_group (links elements shared highlighting); discrete color/shape legends become interactive automatically. plot declares none renders static — still embeddable — SVG.
code: library(quill) library(gloss)  df <- data.frame(wt = mtcars$wt, mpg = mtcars$mpg, model = rownames(mtcars))  vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, tooltip = model, data_id = model) |>   as_widget()

title: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
text: gloss depends vellum’s scene_svg() / scene_model() contract, wraps vellum scene, whoever produced . quill Suggests (examples/tests).

title: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
text: JS runtime TypeScript srcts/, bundled esbuild committed inst/htmlwidgets/gloss.js (R package installs Node):
code: npm install            # esbuild + typescript (+ jsdom for tests) npm run build          # srcts/index.ts -> inst/htmlwidgets/gloss.js node tests/js/behavior.test.js   # headless DOM behaviour suite

title: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
text: Hover/selection appearance customisable two levels compose — widget-wide theme per-element grammar colours. accept R CSS colour; per-element colours win theme, wins built-defaults. 1. Widget theme — as_widget() arguments set look whole plot: 2. Per-element (data-driven) grammar — declare hover_color / selected_color mark, just like tooltip/data_id. can constants mapped column, different marks highlight differently: layers use CSS-variable mechanism, plot can set theme default override per element pipe. Anything set falls back built-look. (can also override raw CSS classes — .gloss-hl, [data-key].gloss-selected, .gloss-tip, … — host document, arguments supported API.)
code: library(quill) library(gloss)  df <- data.frame(wt = mtcars$wt, mpg = mtcars$mpg, model = rownames(mtcars))  vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, tooltip = model, data_id = model) |>   as_widget(     hover_color    = "steelblue",  # outline on hover (default: none, just dim-others)     selected_color = "orange",     # outline on click-select     dim_opacity    = 0.15          # how much the non-hovered marks fade (default 0.28)   ) df$cyl <- factor(mtcars$cyl)  vplot(df) |>   mark_point(     x = wt, y = mpg, data_id = model,     hover_color    = ifelse(mtcars$cyl == 8, "firebrick", "steelblue"),     selected_color = "black"   ) |>   as_widget()          # a per-element colour overrides any widget theme

title: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
text: Selection can linked across views data key — select brush one plot data highlight everywhere. bus (gloss ↔︎ gloss, dependency). Give widgets shared group: Selection projects field: marks declare hover_group, clicking one element selects whole series (select one cylinder count → cars). crosstalk (interop plotly, leaflet, DT, filter_* inputs). Wrap data crosstalk SharedData (key must match data_id) pass as_widget(): gloss uses selection engine layers crosstalk top optional bridge (SelectionHandle + FilterHandle), crosstalk filter hides non-matching marks (display-tier cross-filter) selection round-trips widgets. crosstalk client library pulled pass SharedData.
code: df$hp <- mtcars$hp  p1 <- vplot(df) |> mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, data_id = model) |> as_widget(group = "cars") p2 <- vplot(df) |> mark_point(x = hp, y = qsec, data_id = model) |> as_widget(group = "cars") # in an HTML doc, selecting a point in p1 highlights the same car in p2 library(crosstalk) sd <- SharedData$new(df, key = ~model, group = "cars")  bscols(   vplot(sd$origData()) |> mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, data_id = model) |> as_widget(crosstalk = sd),   DT::datatable(sd)      # selecting rows / points links both ways ) filter_slider("hp", "Horsepower", sd, ~hp)   # crosstalk's filter inputs hide non-matching marks

title: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
text: plot maps discrete color shape scale declares interactivity (e.g. data_id), legend swatch becomes handle whole data series — extra arguments needed: quill tags swatch series drives mark series membership; gloss projects swatch event onto every mark series, reusing highlight/select machinery hover_group. Selecting via swatch also links across views crosstalk, exactly like selecting mark.
code: df <- data.frame(   wt = mtcars$wt, mpg = mtcars$mpg,   model = rownames(mtcars), cyl = factor(mtcars$cyl) ) vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, color = cyl, data_id = model) |>   as_widget() # hover the "6" swatch -> all six-cylinder cars highlight (the swatch stays lit); # click it -> the whole series is selected (respecting single/multiple mode).

title: Interactive web widgets for vellum scenes
text: gloss interactivity layer small ecosystem packages share vellum scene model: vellum — parchment: low-level graphics backend (Rust scene graph, PNG/SVG/PDF renderer). quill — pen: pipe-first grammar graphics compiles plot spec vellum scene. gloss — gloss: package. scriptorium — installs loads whole ecosystem one step.

what: Turn a vellum scene (or quill plot) into an interactive widget — as_widget
title: Turn a vellum scene (or quill plot) into an interactive widget — as_widget
text: as_widget() terminal verb interactivity pipeline: compiles input vellum scene, emits SVG (per-element data-keys) vellum::scene_model() element table, bundles gloss JavaScript runtime self-contained htmlwidgets::createWidget() widget. result hover tooltips, hover highlighting, click selection entirely client-side — Shiny, server round-trip. Pan/zoom, brush, selection work mouse, touch (drag pan, two-finger pinch zoom), keyboard input: arrows pan +/-/0 zoom reset, except accessibility (default) arrows move marks one focused — see a11y argument.

title: Turn a vellum scene (or quill plot) into an interactive widget — as_widget
code: as_widget(   x,   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   tooltip = TRUE,   hover = TRUE,   select = TRUE,   brush = TRUE,   zoom = TRUE,   toolbar = TRUE,   nearest = TRUE,   a11y = TRUE,   alt = NULL,   hover_color = NULL,   selected_color = NULL,   dim_opacity = NULL,   tooltip_style = NULL,   export_filename = NULL,   export_scale = NULL,   group = NULL,   crosstalk = NULL,   select_mode = c("multiple", "single"),   elementId = NULL )

title: Turn a vellum scene (or quill plot) into an interactive widget — as_widget
text:
x quill plot (PlotSpec / PlotComposition) vellum scene — anything vellum::as_vellum_scene() accepts. width, height Widget size (valid CSS size, NULL size scene). Passed htmlwidgets::createWidget(). tooltip, hover, select Toggles three hover/click interactions (TRUE). brush, zoom, toolbar Toggles rectangular brush-select, wheel/drag pan-zoom (via SVG viewBox), -hover toolbar (TRUE). nearest TRUE (default), hover snaps nearest mark within small radius cursor directly one (helps sparse points). a11y Accessibility (default TRUE). Makes widget keyboard- screen-reader-navigable chart: SVG labelled interactive chart (role="graphics-document"), mark focusable graphics-symbol roving tabindex (arrow keys move marks, Enter/Space select, Escape exits), polite aria-live region announces focused/selected mark, visually-hidden data table lists every mark assistive tech. FALSE restores previous behaviour (chart semantics, marks focusable). alt Accessible label (alt text) chart whole. Defaults scene's title/description — quill sets automatically plot title quill::plot_alt() — explicit value needed raw vellum scene override. hover_color, selected_color Outline colours hovered / selected elements (R CSS colour), applied widget-wide. hover_color = NULL (default) keeps plain dim-others hover; selected_color = NULL uses built-default. per-mark hover_color/selected_color declared quill overrides mark. dim_opacity Opacity (0–1) non-hovered elements hovering (default 0.28); NULL keeps default. tooltip_style Optional named list styling tooltip box: background / color (R CSS colour), fontsize, max_width (CSS length). NULL (default) uses built-style. Tooltip text rendered safe HTML — author-built tooltip = (e.g. via glue()) may use /<>/
bold/italic/line breaks; data values escaped inert tags honoured (scripts/attributes). export_filename, export_scale download filename base (extension; default "plot") PNG resolution multiplier (default 1) toolbar's SVG/PNG export. Exports capture current (zoomed/panned) view. group Optional linking group name. Widgets sharing group link client-side: selecting (brushing) one highlights data keys others — Shiny, crosstalk. Selection projects hover_group marks declare (select one, select whole series). crosstalk Optional crosstalk::SharedData (crosstalk group name string) link widget crosstalk ecosystem (plotly, leaflet, DT, crosstalk's filter_* inputs). widget's data_ids must match SharedData's keys. crosstalk filter hides non-matching elements (display-tier cross-filter). Requires crosstalk package. select_mode "multiple" (default; click toggles element) "single" (click replaces selection). elementId Optional explicit widget DOM id.

title: Turn a vellum scene (or quill plot) into an interactive widget — as_widget
text: htmlwidget class "gloss".

title: Turn a vellum scene (or quill plot) into an interactive widget — as_widget
text: Interactivity driven keys/metadata plot declares. quill come reserved data_id / tooltip / hover_group mark arguments; plot declares none renders static (still embeddable) SVG. hovered element data_id tooltip shows key. scene metadata gloss reads — vellum::scene_model() element table SVG data-key / data-vellum-* attributes — specified vellum's "scene contract" vignette (vignette("scene-contract", package = "vellum")).

title: Turn a vellum scene (or quill plot) into an interactive widget — as_widget
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(quill) df <- data.frame(wt = mtcars$wt, mpg = mtcars$mpg, model = rownames(mtcars)) vplot(df) |>   mark_point(x = wt, y = mpg, tooltip = model, data_id = model) |>   as_widget() } # }

what: gloss: Interactive Web Widgets for Vellum Scenes — gloss-package
title: gloss: Interactive Web Widgets for Vellum Scenes — gloss-package
text: Turns 'vellum' scene ('quill' plot) self-contained, client-side interactive HTML widget: hover tooltips highlighting, click selection, rectangular brush-select, pan/zoom, toolbar – driven per-element data keys, bounding boxes, 'scene_model()' metadata 'vellum' emits. Requires Shiny server round-trip. Named manuscript gloss – annotation revealed page.

what: Shiny bindings for gloss widgets — gloss-shiny
title: Shiny bindings for gloss widgets — gloss-shiny
text: Standard htmlwidgets output/render helpers gloss widget can appear Shiny app interactive R Markdown document.

title: Shiny bindings for gloss widgets — gloss-shiny
code: glossOutput(outputId, width = "100%", height = "400px")  renderGloss(expr, env = parent.frame(), quoted = FALSE)

title: Shiny bindings for gloss widgets — gloss-shiny
text: outputId Shiny output slot id. width, height Widget size. expr expression producing gloss widget. env, quoted Standard non-standard-evaluation plumbing.

title: Shiny bindings for gloss widgets — gloss-shiny
text: glossOutput(): Shiny output UI element. renderGloss(): Shiny render function.

title: Shiny bindings for gloss widgets — gloss-shiny
text: widget rendered glossOutput("plot") reports user's interactions back server reactive inputs, keyed output id. values element data keys (data_id quill mark declares); map back data key. input$plot_selected Character vector currently selected keys (click / brush / keyboard, selection arriving linked widget). Updates state — re-selecting set -op. character(0) nothing selected. input$plot_click list list(key=) click; key NULL click empty space. event input — fires every click, even mark twice. input$plot_hover hovered key, NULL pointer leaves mark. Updates state (re-fires hovered key changes). input$plot_brush list list(keys=, x0=, y0=, x1=, y1=) brush gesture completes: selected keys brushed rectangle scene's device-pixel (viewBox) coordinates. event input. emitted inside live Shiny session; static render (knitr, pkgdown, htmltools::save_html()) produces identical output input traffic. Driving widget server (setting selection without re-render) planned addition.

title: gloss (development version)
text: Shiny input read-back. widget rendered glossOutput() now reports user’s interactions server reactive inputs keyed output id: input$<id>_selected (selected keys, state), input$<id>_click (list(key=), event), input$<id>_hover (hovered key NULL, state), input$<id>_brush (list(keys=, x0=, y0=, x1=, y1=), event). Values element data keys, map straight back data. Emitted live Shiny session — static render unchanged produces input traffic. See new Using gloss Shiny article. (Driving widget server — gloss_proxy() — planned follow-.) Accessibility (a11y = TRUE, default). interactive widget now keyboard- screen-reader-navigable, mute image: SVG announced interactive chart (role="graphics-document" + aria-roledescription), labelled scene’s title/description (quill sets automatically) explicit as_widget(alt =); every mark focusable graphics-symbol roving tabindex — arrow keys move marks, Enter/Space toggles selection, Escape exits; polite aria-live region announces focused / selected mark; visually-hidden data table lists every mark assistive tech. gated a11y; a11y = FALSE restores previous output exactly. See quill Accessibility article.

title: gloss 0.2.0
text:
Interaction-depth release (ROADMAP §4). Rich tooltips. Tooltip text now renders safe HTML: author-built tooltip = (e.g. via glue()) may use /<>/
bold/italic/ line breaks. Data values escaped inert, attribute-free tags honoured — scripts, handlers, attributes (XSS). New as_widget(tooltip_style =) themes tooltip box (background/color/fontsize/ max_width). Touch + keyboard. widget driven pointer events, pan/brush/ hover work mouse, touch, pen one path; two-finger pinch zooms. widget focused, arrow keys pan, +/- zoom, 0 resets. Configurable export. as_widget(export_filename =, export_scale =) set download filename base hi-res PNG multiplier; exports capture current (zoomed/panned) view. “copy PNG clipboard” toolbar button appears Clipboard API available. Large-N performance. Hover/selection highlighting uses per-render key→node cache instead querySelectorAll per key, nearest-mark hover scan throttled one per animation frame. New “Interactive widgets: tour” article. Deferred: spatial index hit-testing (DOM cost real bottleneck, now cached; bbox scans cheap datashade collapses huge clouds raster); coordinated zoom across linked widgets URL deep-linking; PDF export.

title: gloss 0.1.0
text: First release. gloss turns vellum scene quill plot self-contained, client-side interactive HTML widget via single terminal as_widget() pipe — Shiny, server round-trip. Everything ships first release.

title: gloss 0.1.0
text: as_widget() compiles quill plot raw vellum scene bundles SVG + scene_model() element table htmlwidget. Hover — tooltip (declared tooltip, falling back key) highlight inverse-dim; hovered element’s hover_group highlights whole group. Hover snaps nearest mark cursor isn’t directly one. Click — select (single / multiple modes); every element sharing key toggles together. Brush — drag rectangle select every element covers. Pan / zoom — mouse wheel pan-mode drag reframe SVG viewBox, plus zoom--selection reset. Toolbar (hover) — brush/pan mode toggle, zoom--selection, reset zoom, download SVG, download PNG, fullscreen. Everything opt-outable via as_widget() arguments (tooltip/hover/select/brush/zoom/toolbar/nearest). Legend interaction. discrete color/shape scale interactive plot, legend swatch drives whole data series: hovering swatch highlights series (swatch stays lit), clicking selects series (links across views / crosstalk). Automatic — extra arguments. Linked views. as_widget(group=) links gloss widgets client-side (dependency): selecting/brushing one highlights data keys others, projecting hover_group (select one, select series). as_widget(crosstalk = SharedData) bridges crosstalk ecosystem (plotly / leaflet / DT filter_* inputs) via SelectionHandle + FilterHandle; crosstalk filter hides non-matching marks (display-tier cross-filter). crosstalk Suggests, loaded used. Widget theme — as_widget(hover_color=, selected_color=, dim_opacity=) sets look whole plot (R CSS colour). Per-element grammar — quill marks’ hover_color/selected_color (constant column-mapped) style element individually override theme. use CSS variables defaults ← theme ← per-element cascade. JS runtime TypeScript srcts/, bundled esbuild committed inst/htmlwidgets/gloss.js (R package installs Node).
